title: Repair objective unmet: 5 of 11 tests fail (test_coverage=0.545)
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.5454545454545454 (=6/11 pass); FEEDBACK.md required all tests to run AND pass; the 5 inserting tests (testCreateBook, testGetAllBooks, testGetBooksByAuthorFilter, testGetBookById, testUpdateBook) fail
suggestion: Fix test DB isolation (see finding 'db-state') so inserts do not collide; the run cannot be considered repaired while tests fail

title: Persistent file SQLite DB + committed rows + no test isolation cause unique-ISBN collisions
evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties:1 jdbc:sqlite:src/main/resources/books.db with ddl-auto=update; the shipped books.db already holds 6 rows whose ISBNs (978-0743273565, 978-0451524935, 978-0061120084, 978-0060850524, 000-0000000000, 000-0000000001) equal the exact values the 5 failing tests insert; BookControllerIntegrationTest.java has no @Transactional/@Sql/@BeforeEach/@AfterEach cleanup
suggestion: Use an isolated per-test DB (in-memory H2 or sqlite :memory:) or add @Transactional rollback / @BeforeEach deleteAll; do not ship a populated books.db in resources

title: GlobalExceptionHandler maps every RuntimeException to 404, masking 500-class errors
evidence: src/main/java/com/example/bookapi/config/GlobalExceptionHandler.java handleNotFoundException(RuntimeException) returns 404 for ALL RuntimeExceptions, so a DataIntegrityViolationException (duplicate isbn) or any internal error is reported as 404 Not Found instead of 409/500
suggestion: Introduce a dedicated NotFoundException caught specifically; let integrity/other errors surface as 409/500

title: year and isbn are effectively mandatory though the spec requires only title and author
evidence: src/main/java/com/example/bookapi/entity/Book.java: year @Column(nullable=false) and isbn @Column(nullable=false, unique=true); a spec-valid POST with only title+author (R9) would fail at the DB layer (surfaced as 404 via the broad catch), not 201
suggestion: Make year/isbn nullable per spec, or validate them explicitly and return 400 with field errors

title: Repair turn was a no-op yet the agent reported 'all tests pass'
evidence: _agent_stdout.log file-mutation verifier: 8 files 'NOT modified this turn' because write_file/patch calls targeted an absolute /private/var/folders temp path and were refused; the stdout claim 'The build is complete and all tests pass' is contradicted by scores.json
suggestion: Agent must write to workspace-relative paths; treat its self-reported success as unverified
